project: PRD: Fusion CRM v4 — Full Rebuild
description: Rebuild Fusion CRM from the legacy MySQL/PHP v3 system into a modern, AI-native real estate CRM using the Laravel Starter Kit (Inertia + React) as the only base. The new app runs on Laravel 12, PostgreSQL (with pgvector), Inertia v2 + React 19, Filament v5, and Reverb. It replaces the lead-centric data model with a unified Contact entity, adds AI agents, RAG, and Thesys C1 generative UI, and migrates all legacy data from MySQL.

userStories.title: Bootstrap the application
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to bootstrap the Fusion CRM v4 app from the Laravel Starter Kit so that all subsequent steps build on a correctly configured foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Read rebuild-plan/00-ui-design-system.md in full before implementing
Brand tokens applied to resources/css/app.css (primary #f28036, warm #f3f1ee, fusion-dark vars per §2)
fusion and fusion-dark theme presets created in config/theme.php
.env contains THEME_PRESET=fusion, THEME_FONT=geist-sans, APP_ENV=local, APP_DEBUG=true, QUEUE_CONNECTION=sync, MAIL_MAILER=log
mysql_legacy database connection configured in config/database.php (for import steps)
PostgreSQL migrations run successfully (php artisan migrate --force)
Base seeders run (php artisan db:seed)
php artisan env:validate and php artisan app:health pass without errors

All Inertia pages and DataTables follow design principles, typography, spacing, interactive states, animation, form conventions, DataTable conventions, icon rules, and anti-patterns from 00-ui-design-system.md §§5–15, 19, 21

userStories.title: Projects and lots with import
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to implement the projects and lots data model and migrate all legacy project/lot data so that property data is available before contacts are added.
acceptanceCriteria: SPEED MODE ACTIVE: Do NOT run composer test, composer test:quick, pest, or php artisan test — tests hang for 5+ minutes. Skip all test runs, log 'tests skipped (speed mode)' and advance immediately.
Read rebuild-plan/00-import-command-templates.md before writing any fusion:import-* command
Projects and lots models, migrations, factories, seeders created per 00-database-design.md
Filament resources for projects and lots created and functional
DataTable views for projects and lots created per 00-component-map.md
Flyer PDF generator implemented (spatie/laravel-pdf v2) per 00-websites-puck-builder.md
fusion:import-projects-lots command follows the base command template (handle, mapRow, upsertRow, dry-run, chunking, idempotency)
fusion:verify-import-projects-lots passes with expected row counts from rebuild-plan/11-verification-per-step.md
Migrations run: php artisan migrate --force

Contact model with full relationships, Scout Searchable trait, and toSearchableArray() implemented
CRM roles seeded per rebuild-plan/00-roles-permissions.md (all roles from superadmin → referral_partner, org-scoped)
All permissions from the permission catalogue seeded and assigned per the role→permission matrix
Filament resource and DataTable for contacts created and functional
fusion:import-contacts builds the lead_id → contact_id map (stored for subsequent import steps)
fusion:verify-import-contacts passes with expected row counts

userStories.title: Two-way MySQL ↔ PostgreSQL sync infrastructure
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to write production-ready two-way sync scripts between legacy MySQL and new PostgreSQL so that the systems can coexist during a phased cutover if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: SPEED MODE ACTIVE: Do NOT run composer test, composer test:quick, pest, or php artisan test — tests hang for 5+ minutes. Skip all test runs, log 'tests skipped (speed mode)' and advance immediately.

Scheduler entry for both commands added (every 30 minutes) but left disabled unless coexistence is explicitly required
Conflict resolution rules implemented per the step file
Dry-run (--dry-run) of both commands produces a log entry confirming both directions work without errors
Scripts are production-ready but sync is opt-in
